title: Booking a Holiday Cottage in the Lake District

turns.speaker: Rachel
turns.text: Good afternoon, Lakeland Cottage Holidays, Rachel speaking. How can I help?

turns.speaker: Simon
turns.text: Hello. I'm hoping to book a cottage for a short break in the summer, for my family.

turns.speaker: Rachel
turns.text: Lovely, I'd be happy to help with that. Let me take a few details first. Could I have your surname, please?

turns.speaker: Simon
turns.text: Yes, it's Whitaker.

turns.speaker: Rachel
turns.text: Could you spell that for me?

turns.speaker: Simon
turns.text: Certainly. It's W-H-I-T-A-K-E-R. Whitaker. First name Simon.

turns.speaker: Rachel
turns.text: Thank you, Simon. And when were you thinking of coming?

turns.speaker: Simon
turns.text: We'd like to arrive on Friday the fourteenth of August, and stay for four nights.

turns.speaker: Rachel
turns.text: Friday the fourteenth of August, four nights — that's leaving on the Tuesday. And how many people will there be?

turns.speaker: Simon
turns.text: There'll be four of us — two adults and two children.

turns.speaker: Rachel
turns.text: Perfect. I've got two cottages free that week. The first is Foxglove Cottage, which sleeps four and costs ninety-five pounds a night. The second is Bluebell Barn, which is a bit bigger — it sleeps six and costs a hundred and twenty pounds a night.

turns.speaker: Simon
turns.text: And whereabouts are they? I'd love to be near the water.

turns.speaker: Rachel
turns.text: Foxglove Cottage is in Keswick — oh, sorry, let me correct that, it's actually in Ambleside, right by the lake. Bluebell Barn is the one in Keswick, a little further from the shore.

turns.speaker: Simon
turns.text: In that case Foxglove Cottage sounds perfect — the one in Ambleside, by the lake. And four beds is plenty for us.

turns.speaker: Rachel
turns.text: Excellent choice. So that's Foxglove Cottage, four nights at ninety-five pounds a night. I should mention there's a one-off cleaning charge of thirty pounds added at the end.

turns.speaker: Simon
turns.text: That's fine. Do you need anything to hold the booking?

turns.speaker: Rachel
turns.text: Yes, we take a deposit of one hundred and fifty pounds to secure it, with the balance due two weeks before you arrive. Now, one thing to note — the cottage doesn't allow pets, so if you were hoping to bring a dog, I'm afraid that one's not suitable.

turns.speaker: Simon
turns.text: No, no pets, that's fine. What time can we check in?

turns.speaker: Rachel
turns.text: Check-in is from four o'clock in the afternoon, and we ask guests to check out by ten in the morning. I'll email you the key-safe code the day before. Your booking reference is B, then two-nine-six-one — so B2961.

turns.speaker: Simon
turns.text: B2961 — got it. Thank you so much, Rachel, that's brilliant.
